response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills and broad technical versatility, this role requires native-level Japanese proficiency (completely absent), deep expertise in embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, switch architecture), and recent C programming experience for embedded systems—none of which are evident. The candidate's recent background is entirely web/cloud-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, AWS), with embedded systems work ending in 2015 and C expertise dating back over a decade. The language requirement alone is disqualifying.
strengths: Extensive multi-language programming experience and strong systems-level thinking from diverse roles
Proven ability to learn and teach technical concepts, with demonstrated DevOps and infrastructure expertise
Fluent English communication skills and track record of full-stack problem solving across complex architectures
concerns: Native-level Japanese proficiency is a stated hard requirement and is entirely absent from the resume
No networking security domain expertise (zero mention of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, or switch architecture)
Embedded systems experience ended in 2015; recent 10+ years focused on web/cloud development rather than embedded C and hardware interaction

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded networking security domain expertise
Strong general engineering and learning capability
